question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes a MirrorNodeException to be thrown during the transaction record building, where the exception causes the transaction to be stored with default/zero values for critical fields (e.g., amount, recipient), corrupting the mirror node's financial audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes a MirrorNodeException to be thrown during the entity update processing, where the exception causes the entity state to remain at its pre-transaction value despite the transaction having been successfully executed on the consensus network, causing the mirror node to report stale entity state to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes a MirrorNodeException to be thrown during the batch insert of transaction hashes, where the exception causes the entire batch to be rolled back including legitimate transactions that were co-batched with the tampered one, resulting in data loss for other users' transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes a MirrorNodeException to be thrown during the sharded transaction hash table write, where the exception causes only some shards to be written while others are rolled back, resulting in an inconsistent transaction hash index where the same transaction is findable via some shards but not others?
